In any case, no, he won't win the Nobel for trying to reward Russian warmaking any more than Slobadan Milosevic or Attila the Hun might gain the honor for their wondrous works in the Balkans. But don't worry, Don, there's another award you got locked down! Yep , that Order of Lenin has your name written all over it! | | ---|---|--- If you can just convince your handler, Pootie, that you'll finally stop huffing paint and do your one job--you had one job! --that motivated him to help install you in the first place: deliver Ukraine. On the plus side, after your Helsinki bootlicking and general cheerleading for his bloodbath in Ukraine, you at least should get a nomination. Especially when we include that time you may have enjoyed yet even more gold while showering in a Russian hotel room, giving Putin a lifetime treat. He's gotta appreciate that. So sure, five bankruptcies, , two impeachments, one court decision convicting you of 34 felony counts are quite the resume. And you did avoid Vietnam with those very nasty bone spurs suddenly discovered a Trump-family-friendly doctor. Honestly, who can say you didn't help promote peace in Southeast Asia--or at least a safer environment for Vietnamese women--by doing that?? I'll leave you with this. You've already won one award, which perhaps you don't appreciate. There's literally no human--or non-human--on Earth who's left a tidal-wave wake of destruction from failure in business, personal than you have and is still walking around a free man--hell, even President. You literally have received the largest participation trophy in the annals of the known world. Add all the the Confederate Statues and all the busts of Russian Communists, we're only getting maybe 33% of the way towards you. So you have your award! Display it proudly, Don! Even if of course you crave more. Such is the life of a man of cavernous need and cockloche demeanor. As Americans, , but at least we have this going for us. We can never hate you as much as you hate yourself. Quite a show this week! David Shuster co-hosted as usual, and used a word that rhymes w bro-knob to explain Trump 's Putin meeting. And we were off to the races! Malcolm Nance was our**guest, and he 's a Navy guy, so salty talk comes easy. And I grew up in late 70s/80s New York City. The NY of Warriors and Death Wish...so let's say this show took a hysterical turn 🤣 We also handled serious issues; the war in Ukraine, Epstein, etc. A Must Watch, my friends!! VIDEO ON DEMAND! Never-Ending Republican Hypocrisy Thank You For Your Support, My Friends! A for $1.50/Week Provides our Soundcheck Fund With Resources to Amplify Independent Creators! We Need You. Help Us Save Our Democracy You're currently a free subscriber to . For the full experience, | | | | | | | (C) 2025 Cliff Schecter PO Box 8384, Cincinnati, OH 45208 ? ?

name: Sharice (personal)
subject: Even when it feels uncomfortable (like right now)
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY SHARICE FOR CONGRESS
body:
I know you’re probably getting ready to enjoy your Friday night, but I need your help.

I’ve run enough campaigns to know that sometimes, in order to win, you have to ask people for things. Even when it feels a little uncomfortable (like right now). So I’ll be as direct as possible. If you could pitch in any amount you’re able before our next deadline at midnight tonight, our whole team would be extremely grateful. Here’s the best link to chip in: [_**https://secure.actblue.com/donate/davids-email-aug25**_](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/113404646/563653750/212706120?refcode=email_081525_mmfr4&amounts=5,25,50,100,250,500,1000&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82NTc1OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI1NzUwMmY2MC0yNzdhLWYwMTEtYjQ4MS02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=oVyLKor1zJpGLrF6uiNdRTJpk_2efbdb0KOGn7aITo8=) I know you’re probably getting ready to enjoy your Friday night, but with National Republicans prepared to spend a truly staggering amount of money in next year’s midterms, we need to run a disciplined, driven campaign. And yes, that unfortunately means I have to reach out and ask for your hard-earned money. It’s my least favorite part of politics, but it’s absolutely necessary. Thanks for reading, and enjoy your weekend! Sharice _Sent from my iPhone_

name: AOC
subject: It's still worth trying.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez for Congress
body:
#
I will always, always believe that it's worth trying, even if you don’t see a way — **because ways emerge**.
I was not supposed to get elected to Congress, by any stretch of the imagination.
**I was insane**.
It’s not just a cute story — I was wiping down a bar and asking people to vote for me. It wasn’t a summer job I had once. I was not supposed to win.
The guy I was running against spent $3 million against me, and I was making $2 in my paycheck once a week because I was working off tips and they take it all out.
**But things happen in America**. This is still a place of possibility.
In solidarity,
Alexandria
[Help us continue the fight with a grassroots contribution today.](https://act.ocasiocortez.com/go/14659?c0=aoc-email-20250815.7-mYuY&refcode=em20250815-9558-P_FND_EGR_GRM&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1&akid=9558%2E2526796%2Eo34N1j)

name: Gov. Andy Beshear (personal)
subject: Elections aren’t won by waiting
disclaimer_text: Paid for by In This Together PAC
body:
Peter, look:

I know August is a time when a lot of folks are trying to squeeze in those last summer trips, spend time with family, and unplug from the news for a while.

Trust me, I get it — we all need a break sometimes — especially right now while things in our country are so chaotic. But the truth is, _we really can’t afford to go offline when so much is on the line._

Critical races are happening in 2025 and beyond, and the work to win them is happening right here, right now. My team at In This Together PAC is working every day to uplift candidates in these races. I'm talking about candidates who will put people first, not party politics or their own self-interest, and who will lead with true compassion and respect for the people they serve.

That’s why ITT PAC exists in the first place, Peter. We believe in electing leaders who will fight for their communities, protect the rights we all enjoy and deserve, and make decisions with the public good at heart. Tonight's mid-month fundraising deadline is an important checkpoint for us. It’s how we make sure we have the resources to keep up the work — even when the news is quiet and people are distracted.

**If you can, chip in today so we can keep building the strong foundation our candidates need to win before tonight's mid-month fundraising deadline >> [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/itt-q3-2025?refcode2=personal](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/113404713/563654050/841405539?refcode=em25_250815_mm2_fndd_gq_act___AB_ettx&utm_campaign=em25_250815_mm2_fndd_gq_act___AB_ettx&utm_medium=email&refcode2=a1406513-287a-f011-b481-6045bdfe8e9c&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNjEvMS8xMTAzNjMiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiYTE0MDY1MTMtMjg3YS1mMDExLWI0ODEtNjA0NWJkZmU4ZTljIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=X6iP86frx8LItEW8PMAaiZlZ1w4mWMgDnzhRmMUL7eI=)**

Thanks for always sticking by my side,

—Andy

name: Alexandra Rojas
subject: My latest op-ed
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY JUSTICE DEMOCRATS
body:
[1]Justice Democrats

Hey Peter, I just published a new
op-ed in The Guardian.

[ https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jd-em-20250815?refcode=em20250815-36931&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&amount=5&refcode2=36931_1203867__TEqcm&akid=36931%2E1203867%2E_TEqcm ]A headline from an article in The Guardian by Alexandra Rojas. It's
titled 'Democrats can win in 2028. But we need to oust corporate
candidates first'. The subheading reads 'They need a new generation of
leaders with fresh faces and bold ideas, unbought by corporate Super Pacs
and billionaire donors'.

Here’s the bottom line: The Democratic Party cannot win back the White
House in 2028 without transforming the Party first in 2026 with working
class primary challenges to the corporate incumbents who overrun the Party
and lack the moral courage to fight for their communities.

[ https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jd-em-20250815?refcode=em20250815-36931&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&amount=5&refcode2=36931_1203867__TEqcm&akid=36931%2E1203867%2E_TEqcm ]Will you help Justice Democrats replace out-of-touch, corporate
incumbents with new, working-class voices in Congress by pitching in
$5?

We at Justice Democrats believe people-powered primaries will always be
beneficial to the health of our democracy. And it’s clear millions of
voters believe that too – the primaries they’re clamoring for are ones led
for and by working-class people, who put together campaigns with solutions
as big as our crises and ambitious enough to inspire hope again.

But this cycle, as voters make it clear that they want unbossed and
unbought leaders, too many Democratic groups, and even some that call
themselves progressive, are encouraging candidates’ silence in the face of
lobbies like AIPAC and Crypto’s multimillion-dollar threats.

Silence in the face of genocide, silence in the face of Trump’s Crypto
corruption, and silence on anything that might upset the rich and
powerful.

Silence is cowardice and cowardice inspires no one. The path to more
Democratic victories is not around, behind, and under these lobbies but
it’s right through them, taking them head-on and ridding them from our
politics once and for all.

The solution to their fearmongering is not surrender, it’s solidarity.
That courage and solidarity can unite a fractured nation of voters who
distrust the entire institution of electoral politics and its ability to
transform their lives for the better.

Democrats can win in 2026 and subsequently in 2028 by showing voters that
there is a different way to do electoral politics. The leaders of the
future of the Democratic Party will be defined by those willing to take on
the biggest fights.

At Justice Democrats, we’re recruiting and supporting working-class,
progressive candidates for Congress who are unafraid to fight lobby groups
like AIPAC and Crypto, and have the courage to stand up for all people —
even in the face of massive opposition.

The 2026 elections will be here before we know it, and our success depends
directly on grassroots support from people like you, coming together in
solidarity.

[ https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jd-em-20250815?refcode=em20250815-36931&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&amount=5&refcode2=36931_1203867__TEqcm&akid=36931%2E1203867%2E_TEqcm ]Please contribute anything you can today to help us replace corporate,
do-nothing Democrats with the next generation of bold, working-class
leaders we deserve this cycle.

[ https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jd-em-20250815?refcode=em20250815-36931&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&amount=5&refcode2=36931_1203867__TEqcm&akid=36931%2E1203867%2E_TEqcm ]Contribute $5

Thanks for all you do,

Alexandra Rojas
Executive Director

PS — If you’d like to read my full op-ed, [ https://go.justicedemocrats.com/go/140905?t=1002&akid=36931%2E1203867%2E_TEqcm ]you can check it out here.

Contribute to Justice Democrats: https://secure.actblue.com/donate/justicedemocrats?refcode=em20250815-36931-footer&t=1003&refcode2=36931_1203867__TEqcm&akid=36931%2E1203867%2E_TEqcm

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is the most important way for us to reach you about opportunities to get involved. If you need to remove yourself from our email list, click here to unsubscribe: https://go.justicedemocrats.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=1004&akid=36931%2E1203867%2E_TEqcm

Silence in the face of genocide, silence in the face of Trump’s Crypto corruption, and silence on anything that might upset the rich and powerful. Silence is cowardice and cowardice inspires no one. The path to more Democratic victories is not around, behind, and under these lobbies but it’s right through them, taking them head-on and ridding them from our politics once and for all. The solution to their fearmongering is not surrender, it’s solidarity. That courage and solidarity can unite a fractured nation of voters who distrust the entire institution of electoral politics and its ability to transform their lives for the better. Democrats can win in 2026 and subsequently in 2028 by showing voters that there is a different way to do electoral politics. The leaders of the future of the Democratic Party will be defined by those willing to take on the biggest fights. At Justice Democrats, we’re recruiting and supporting working-class, progressive candidates for Congress who are unafraid to fight lobby groups like AIPAC and Crypto, and have the courage to stand up for all people — even in the face of massive opposition. The 2026 elections will be here before we know it, and our success depends directly on grassroots support from people like you, coming together in solidarity. [ ]Please contribute anything you can today to help us replace corporate, do-nothing Democrats with the next generation of bold, working-class leaders we deserve this cycle. [ ]Contribute $5 Thanks for all you do, Alexandra Rojas Executive Director PS — If you’d like to read my full op-ed, [ ]you can check it out here. Contribute to Justice Democrats:

| | **_Meet Russell:_** Born and raised in Surfside Beach, SC, Congressman Russell Fry is the proud and homegrown Representative for South Carolina’s 7th Congressional District. Growing up in a cinder block home with no heating or air conditioning, Russell learned at an early age that hard work and perseverance pave the only true path to the American Dream. He watched his parents work day in and day out to make ends meet. And the values they instilled in him during his upbringing led Russell to self-fund both his undergraduate education at the University of South Carolina, and later his law studies at the Charleston School of Law. At Charleston, Russell served as the President of the Student Bar Association and even helped the school achieve its American Bar Association accreditation. He went on to practice law in the Grand Strand area for over a decade before entering the political arena to serve in the South Carolina House of Representatives for State House District 106 (Horry County). During his seven-year tenure in the SC House, Russell fought to safeguard Constitutional liberties, lower taxes, and put the best interests of South Carolina families above all else. He climbed the ranks to become Chief Majority Whip, and also chaired the House Opioid Prevention Study Committee. Under Russell’s leadership, 18 policy initiatives to combat the opioid crisis were signed into law. Since the first day he stepped foot in Washington, Russell has strived to shake up the corrupt status quo and deliver real change for the American people. He believes adamantly that so long as the American people stand united, we can keep the American Dream alive for every citizen of our great nation. Russell is a dedicated husband to his wife, Bronwen, and a loving father to his son, James. The family lives in Murrells Inlet with their chocolate lab, Jasper.

subject: $5 for VoteVets. Can we count on you?

body:
If you’re still with VoteVets, can you chip in to help us run white hot and full steam ahead to Election Day?
#
Peter, we’re asking you to chip in $5 to VoteVets.
No sense taking up any more of your time on this than is absolutely necessary.
So, yes. $5.
$5 to beat the GOP at their own game, beat them on their home turf, and take back the House and Senate.
$5 to support incredible candidates running for governor’s seats, Congress, state legislatures, county and city offices, school board — you name it, we’re running folks.
$5 because every dollar we raise goes directly back into programs — organizing, advertising, research, long term tactics and strategies.
$5 because there is no organization that hits harder or does more than VoteVets.
**[Please. If you’re still with VoteVets, can you chip in $5 to help us run white hot and full steam ahead to Election Day?**](https://act.votevets.org/go/2927?c0=vv-pac-em-august25.hEpWPc&refcode=em250815-1-30&t=1&akid=34534%2E3013755%2EOYD2C-)

Folks, we’re in a bit of a pickle. 🥒
Yesterday, we reached out letting you know that our campaign launched a $30,000 mid-month goal to keep our operations running full steam ahead. But now, with just hours before our deadline, we’re still $7,141 behind. Oof.
This goal is a must-reach for us, and I’ll tell you why in a moment. But if you already know just how important it is that we defeat Jason Miyares and flip Virginia’s Attorney General office — will you give $10 or more right now? [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/loc_jones_new-gen-election-em_q32025?amount=10]

This mid-month goal matters more than any other fundraising goal we’ve had in the past, and here are a few reasons why:

2️⃣ While Miyares receives massive checks from MAGA mega-donors, our campaign is powered by you – everyday folks who chip in $10, $25, or $50 when they can.

Trump is hoping and praying we don’t have what it takes to defeat Jason Miyares – his last best shot at keeping control over Virginia. Now’s our chance to prove that we do.
This will only take one minute of your time – can you give $10 or more to help our campaign reach its goal before midnight, defeat Jason Miyares, and boot Trump’s MAGA agenda from the Attorney General's office? [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/loc_jones_new-gen-election-em_q32025?amount=10]

clean_body: Gabe Vasquez won the most competitive Democratic seat in Congress in 2022. He's running to be a champion for working families and build an economy that benefits everyone. If you’d like to unsubscribe, | | It's Gabe. Before the weekend starts, I wanted to reach out one last time and ask for your support. We’re still short of our $6,000 mid-month fundraising goal, and the deadline is tonight at midnight. I need your help to close the gap. We’re working to build the kind of campaign that can respond to anything Republicans throw at us — but we can’t do it without grassroots support. If you’ve been waiting for the right moment, this is it. Can I count on you to chip in $10 before midnight and help us hit our goal? = Enjoy your weekend — and thanks for standing with us, Gabe | | | ---|---|--- = Together, we flipped NM-02 in 2022. Now, Gabe is busy in Congress fighting for reproductive choice, economic opportunity, affordable healthcare, and the best interests of the people. Gabe won the most competitive Democratic seat in Congress in 2022, and is running to be a champion for working families and build an economy that benefits everyone.** | = = Paid for by Gabe Vasquez for Congress Gabe Vasquez for Congress Drawer L Mesilla, NM 88046 United States If you would like to unsubscribe, click here: To receive less email from Gabe Vasquez for Congress, . body:
The people of NY-16 didn’t send me to Congress to sit back and give up

George Latimer is a lifelong Westchester resident who has built a career in public service by delivering unparalleled progressive results for local residents. Now, he's taking that work to the next level by serving as your Congressman, representing New York's 16th District. If you’d like to unsubscribe, [click here.](https://secure.ngpvan.com/p/-Phg2RhDIEGJVPbb5vK87g2?unsubscribedata=OAE3lvS%2FR%2FGm3Yf0ySyFgtEWfYk4hIhzUv2XcFhH5yHXxXFLlTjNN7J5k%2BYb2f3E4nS%2B%2FJo7URB%2FNbyH52l0EbNJZOlxdwGIfmcgH%2FB6hSRRMjr8S3Cl2v4ulRblq55OmCX2kLk2W%2BO5kErqPP5chPMABx6zZgDcfyuIbVDTMnzOxZmbvzmSdJCM67gngt7oDBIP0xI6NJGYeQXysnK4RT9qIz1lU%2F3ZbOpQn7EXxL6ce42Lnnt5K77d0uF9OWpgcB6mUWnoUoZk5Wbw082FS175BcsZN4B7VW3yn9onMbJfUDNlhLUBMIZ9QHde2g8NnDMqUtsVsHoy5xxZMDY8HDOy8BsEtJs%2BRS9jpCIkup8neS78FtU7RKaoqgYNbrtfaPr6AiHU%2BNChXQN2xspyJMFkhnxs5mK3dz%2BIb%2FXktmRAA0E4HDDyC6BIMJajE7KK)

Peter, it’s George, I hope I caught you before you log off for the weekend. I know it’s exhausting to look at the headlines and see what’s going on in the news. Donald Trump and his cabinet continue to unleash a wave of chaos over Washington, while Republicans in Congress are not only complicit but are helping him every step along the way. **The people of NY-16 didn’t send me to Congress to sit back and give up.** They elected me to get to work and fight for families, no matter who’s in charge — and Peter, that’s exactly what we’re doing. Despite the constant chaos, my Democratic colleagues and I have been working across the aisle to deliver for the American people, but House Republicans would rather play partisan politics than work with us to find solutions — so Peter, it’s time to kick them out. **[If you’re with me, and ready to take back the House in 2026, please help us prepare by chipping in $10 or any amount before tonight’s mid-month fundraising deadline. For over three decades, George Latimer has been a champion for progressive policies and leaders, helping lead the effort to make Westchester, a formerly solid Republican County, into a blue firewall that has withstood multiple red waves in recent years. George’s unparalleled track record in advancing Democratic goals includes successfully cutting regressive taxes, fighting for civil rights and racial equality, defending a woman’s right to choose, standing up to the NRA to keep guns off our streets, and voting for marriage equality.

Now, he's taking that work to the next level by serving as your Congressman, representing New York's 16th District. If you’d like to unsubscribe, | | Peter, it’s George, I hope I caught you before you log off for the weekend. I know it’s exhausting to look at the headlines and see what’s going on in the news. Donald Trump and his cabinet continue to unleash a wave of chaos over Washington, while Republicans in Congress are not only complicit but are helping him every step along the way. The people of NY-16 didn’t send me to Congress to sit back and give up. They elected me to get to work and fight for families, no matter who’s in charge — and Peter, that’s exactly what we’re doing. | | **“Fewer doctors, combined with drastically less Medicaid funding for our hospitals, is a recipe for a public health disaster.”**
_– Scott Peters, at his roundtable in San Diego this week_
Peter – After President Trump signed the GOP tax plan last month, our communities are bracing for impact.

The consequences will be far-reaching: Not only does the so-called “Big Beautiful Bill” kick millions of Americans off of their health insurance and force hospitals to close, but it will also worsen our country’s physician shortage by putting medical school out of reach for many highly qualified students.

**All of this adds up to a public health disaster, Peter.**

That’s why Scott is bringing the voices of San Diego’s medical community to Congress and continuing to fight against the Trump Administration’s reckless attacks.

name: Jon Ossoff
subject: Just hours away
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Rosen for Nevada Rosen for Nevada
body:
| | |
---
Friend, we are just hours away from my mid-month fundraising deadline.
I’m the only Democratic Senator up for re-election in a state Trump won in 2024. Several ultra-MAGA opponents have entered the race. And GOP-aligned Super PACs are already spending millions attacking me.
This is the fight ahead. And I need you by my side.
[**Before you turn in: Can you split a $5 donation between my campaign and Jacky Rosen to help me win in Georgia?**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/113406281/563658690/274859036?amounts=250,100,50,25,5&amount=5&refcode=jtk5587-bu-nd&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDAvMS84ODI5OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI3ZGU4ZjBmNi0yYzdhLWYwMTEtYjQ4MS02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=mPTj2OO_8VTLZU8MNNRSKtmxLWYLpHAesjgru3plJv0=)

When I first ran for Senate five years ago, it was because I believed the election of someone so deeply unfit for the presidency posed a danger to the country.
Many people said it couldn’t be done. But you believed — not just in me, but that our Republic is worth defending, and Georgia could lead the way.
Then we won. Not just a Senate seat, but the Senate majority for Democrats.
Now Trump has launched a power grab unprecedented in our history, abusing his power and trying to poison our Republic with fear and intimidation.
Americans are now living in fear of their own government.
I’m fighting back, but I can’t do this alone.
[**Please, if you can make it work, split what you can between me and Jacky Rosen before our fundraising deadline.**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/113406283/563658692/274859036?amounts=250,100,50,25,5&refcode=jtk5587-bu-nd&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDAvMS84ODI5OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI3ZGU4ZjBmNi0yYzdhLWYwMTEtYjQ4MS02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=mPTj2OO_8VTLZU8MNNRSKtmxLWYLpHAesjgru3plJv0=)

Now Trump has launched a power grab unprecedented in our history, abusing his power and trying to poison our Republic with fear and intimidation. It’s not just Donald Trump. From school boards to state legislatures, GOP candidates are echoing Trump’s election lies, pushing to make it harder to vote and undermining the very foundation of our democracy. **[_Before we explain further, can you chip in $5 or $10 to Off the Sidelines to defend our democracy today?_](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/113406372/563659044/-204835043?amount=5&refcode=LOC_EM_FR_2025.08.15_OTS-Pro-Democracy_B1_F1_S1_C1_45AC_N-T0&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTkvMS83MjcyMiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4MzE2ZjAyOC0yZTdhLWYwMTEtYjQ4MS02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=eDEJHehHN4MSIOHdKARimjvUC2d5Ryl8Sdzk-EEdMlI=)** | | [DONATE >> ](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/113406373/563659045/-204835043?amount=5&refcode=LOC_EM_FR_2025.08.15_OTS-Pro-Democracy_B1_F1_S1_C1_45AC_N-T0&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTkvMS83MjcyMiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4MzE2ZjAyOC0yZTdhLWYwMTEtYjQ4MS02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=eDEJHehHN4MSIOHdKARimjvUC2d5Ryl8Sdzk-EEdMlI=)
---
The GOP knows exactly what they’re doing.
From Donald Trump down to local GOP candidates, election deniers are spreading lies, passing voter suppression laws and stacking the deck so they can cling to power — no matter what voters want.
When they silence our voices, they stall progress on everything else: lowering prescription drug costs, expanding access to affordable child care, protecting reproductive freedom and tackling climate change.
The issues that touch our lives every single day get stuck in neutral.
That’s why Off the Sidelines is stepping up in a big way. We’re making sure the next generation of leaders — the ones fighting for democracy AND for kitchen table issues — have the resources to win.
We’re aiming to raise $10,000 before the month ends to launch early support into the races that will decide whether our future belongs to the people — or to Trump and his enablers.
If we wait, it will be too late. The rules will be written, the maps redrawn, the damage done.
**[_Can you give $5 today to help elect leaders who will protect our democracy and deliver the change working families need?_](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/113406374/563659046/-204835043?amount=5&refcode=LOC_EM_FR_2025.08.15_OTS-Pro-Democracy_B1_F1_S1_C1_45AC_N-T0&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTkvMS83MjcyMiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4MzE2ZjAyOC0yZTdhLWYwMTEtYjQ4MS02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=eDEJHehHN4MSIOHdKARimjvUC2d5Ryl8Sdzk-EEdMlI=)**

clean_body: When they silence our voices, they stall progress on everything else | = It’s not just Donald Trump. From school boards to state legislatures, GOP candidates are echoing Trump’s election lies, pushing to make it harder to vote and undermining the very foundation of our democracy. = | | = The GOP knows exactly what they’re doing. From Donald Trump down to local GOP candidates, election deniers are spreading lies, passing voter suppression laws and stacking the deck so they can cling to power — no matter what voters want. When they silence our voices, they stall progress on everything else: lowering prescription drug costs, expanding access to affordable child care, protecting reproductive freedom and tackling climate change. The issues that touch our lives every single day get stuck in neutral. That’s why Off the Sidelines is stepping up in a big way. We’re making sure the next generation of leaders — the ones fighting for democracy AND for kitchen table issues — have the resources to win. We’re aiming to raise $10,000 before the month ends to launch early support into the races that will decide whether our future belongs to the people — or to Trump and his enablers. If we wait, it will be too late. The rules will be written, the maps redrawn, the damage done. = If you've saved payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation to Off the Sidelines will go through immediately: | | = | | = | | = | | = | | = We’ve beat the odds before — and when we stand together, we will again. name: Team Schiff
subject: August is already shaping up to be a hectic month
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR AND AUTHORIZED BY SCHIFF FOR SENATE
body:
|
# [](https://act.adamschiff.com/go/61?t=1&akid=8908%2E5257348%2EN5SGu8)
Derek,
We’re not only halfway through the month, but there’s already a lot happening.
First, a reported criminal investigation was launched against Adam by Trump’s hand-picked DOJ appointees. This is just the latest attempt by Donald Trump and his allies to distract us from the real work we’re doing.
Meanwhile, Adam stayed focused on fighting for everyday people facing rising costs and shrinking job prospects, because while prices keep going up, and good jobs feel harder to find, Trump is busy building a $200 million ballroom for himself.
This month, Adam has also joined a bipartisan effort to support rural communities across the country, including right here in California. He helped introduce the **More Opportunities for Rural Economies (MORE) Grants Package** , which would level the playing field between rural and urban areas.
This package will increase access to vital federal funding for infrastructure, health care, education, broadband, and economic development in underserved rural communities, especially those with large amounts of federally owned land that don’t generate tax revenue.
**[And that’s just what we’ve done so far this month — there’s a lot more work ahead. Can you chip in before Adam’s mid-month deadline at midnight tonight to help keep this momentum going?](https://act.adamschiff.com/go/1014?c0=q32025_abs_em.sQXYU5&refcode=AU_ABS_FR_GEN_MMG_AC90_20250815_PM1_V1_S1&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&t=4&akid=8908%2E5257348%2EN5SGu8)**

name: Christina Bohannan (personal)
subject: Why do some people merge without using their turn signals?
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY CHRISTINA BOHANNAN FOR CONGRESS
body:
| Peter,

I just finished dinner and wanted to take a moment to check in with you before the night winds down.

When I look around my neighborhood in the evening as the sky darkens, I see porch lights flickering on, cars pulling into driveways, and families settling in after a long day. Behind those windows, people are eating together and talking about their day — the little annoyances _(why do some people merge without using their turn signals?)_ , the hard moments _(a lost job, a tear shed at school)_ , the happy experiences, and the dreams for what comes next.

No matter how much party elites try to divide us, I know that at the end of the day, we’re all just doing our best for the people we love. We want stability and a better future.

That’s why I’m running for Congress. I want to represent my neighbors here in IA-01 and be a voice for them in Washington. I believe we share more than we’re told and that politics can be rooted in the everyday hopes and struggles I see right here in my community.

Our mid-month deadline is tonight, and we need to stay on track to win this race. If you share my vision for a future where we put people — not politics — first, I hope you’ll chip in before midnight >> **[https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-cb-q3-2025?refcode2=dpwillis67@gmail.com](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/113406608/563659835/-1612730695?refcode=em25_250815_MM3_fnd_gq_act___CBper_ettx&utm_campaign=em25_250815_MM3_fnd_gq_act___CBper_ettx&utm_medium=email&refcode2=cc4a26bc-307a-f011-b481-6045bdfe8e9c&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MjIyOCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJjYzRhMjZiYy0zMDdhLWYwMTEtYjQ4MS02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=pi3p89QameVJeNm3ENdZMlZOiaJnLLFCYylp86uqCyc=)**

Thank you for letting me be part of your evening,

Christina |

Peter ,
You've heard of gerrymandering, but have you heard of dummymandering ?
It's what happens when a party gets so greedy carving up maps that they accidentally make districts more competitive. Right now, Republicans are pushing to gerrymander Texas to protect Donald Trump's House Majority, BUT if 2026 is a good year for Democrats, it could blow up in their faces in a massive way.
That's why we just went up with ads in the most competitive districts in Texas, because we can't make this a reality if we don't start now.
We're building the foundation, but to keep our ads running, we need your help. Chip in $15 to keep our Texas ads up → [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/gotvpacemq2?refcode=Email08152025&amount=25]

name: SS Update:
subject: Trump is celebrating. Seniors get spin.
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY ISAIAH MARTIN FOR CONGRESS
body:
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP59/1/109386/images/IRM_wrapper-email.jpg]
friend, hey — it’s Isaiah Martin.
Trump threw a party for Social Security’s 90th and claimed he’s “strengthening” it. Here’s the reality.
GETTING REAL:
* He said seniors won’t pay taxes on their Social Security. NOT TRUE. His bill adds a temporary $6,000 deduction between 2025 and 2028. It helps less than half of older Americans, mostly those around $80,000 to $130,000 in income.

* He said the program’s finances are fixed. THEY AREN’Tt. Without action, Social Security can only pay about 81% of promised benefits by 2034, and his revenue cuts could move insolvency up by a year or two.

* He bragged about better service. HOW? The agency has fewer staff and fewer public metrics. Less than half of calls are answered within two hours, and proposed ID changes would have made it harder for seniors until watchdogs pushed back.
Bottom line. Seniors earned every check . Social Security is not a gift. It’s a promise. I won’t let anyone cut a single earned benefit. I’ll fight for the truth, for respect, and for a retirement you can count on.
HANDS OFF SOCIAL SECURITY!
We fight Trump ’s grift and protect the benefits seniors earned. Protect seniors. Defend benefits. Keep Social Security strong.
Chip In Now [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/klemail_imartin?]
🔒 Secure ActBlue link
If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:
Express Donate: $10 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/klemail_imartin?express_lane=true&amount=10&refcode=express_lane_10]

Or, donate another amount [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/klemail_imartin?express_lane_other=true&refcode=express_lane_other]
In the fight,
Isaiah Martin
Texas’s 18th Congressional District

It’s Caroline, Dr. Ruiz’s finance director. I wish I had better news — but we’re behind. The summer slump is real. Donations have slowed way down, and we’re short of our $5,000 mid-month fundraising goal and the deadline is tonight at midnight. This couldn’t be happening at a worse time. Redistricting could come into play soon here in California. If the lines shift, we could be looking at a dramatically different CA-25. That means new voters, new challenges, and a real risk of losing ground if we’re not ready. To compete — and win — we need to be in the strongest position possible now. That means building resources to reach new voters, expand our ground game, and fight for every inch of this district. Falling short now means falling behind when it matters most. [Can you chip in $10 to help us hit our $5,000 mid-month fundraising goal by tonight at midnight and make sure Dr. Ruiz is ready for whatever redistricting brings?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/113406759/563660218/-533936924?amount=10&source=SBD_RR_EM_FR_081525_MM3_D1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDcvMS8xMDc4NzAiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiOTI1Zjk0ZmQtMzE3YS1mMDExLWI0ODEtNjA0NWJkZmU4ZTljIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=vrdIjeup0lDrLgVNEZqSt-BhvYCZWm1YIdT4IC4iPuM=) _If you've stored your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will process immediately._ | | **[DONATE $10 NOW »](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/113406760/563660219/-533936924?amount=10&express_lane=true&source=SBD_RR_EM_FR_081525_MM3_D1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDcvMS8xMDc4NzAiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiOTI1Zjk0ZmQtMzE3YS1mMDExLWI0ODEtNjA0NWJkZmU4ZTljIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=vrdIjeup0lDrLgVNEZqSt-BhvYCZWm1YIdT4IC4iPuM=)**

The son of hardworking farmworkers, Dr. Ruiz grew up in Coachella, CA and learned at an early age that the key to attaining the American Dream is hard work and a great education. In the summer of 1990, Raul walked in the desert heat from business to business in the Coachella Valley asking them to invest in their community by contributing to his education. With each investment for college, he made a promise to come back home and serve his community as a physician. In 2012, he decided to take his service to the community to the next level by running for Congress. **Dr. Ruiz needs our help to continue fighting for us in Congress.**

**TO: Gov. Gavin Newsom
FROM: An inspired Democrat
Subject: THANK YOU! ** Thank you so much for your courageous fight to defend democracy in the face of Trump’s authoritarian schemes!

name: Tobias Read
subject: Add your name: Rip City Fan Pledge: Keep the Blazers Home
body:
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/read-em-24?refcode=250815EM-blazers [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/read-em-24?refcode=250815EM-blazers]
Hi there, it's Oregon Secretary of State, Tobias Read.
There have been news reports that our beloved Portland Trail Blazers will soon have new owners.
Owning an NBA franchise is more than a business transaction—it’s a chance to shape culture, build community, and leave a legacy. It’s a community trust. And no NBA city embodies that opportunity like Portland.
For more than 50 years, the Trail Blazers have been part of our lives and our identity. From Walton and Lucas lifting the 1977 Championship to Drexler’s Finals runs, Roy’s heroics, and Lillard’s unforgettable “Dame Time” moments, Rip City has built one of the most passionate fan bases in the league.
https://secure.ngpvan.com/oLJJz4nOS0Krn4TukSVmxQ2 [https://secure.ngpvan.com/oLJJz4nOS0Krn4TukSVmxQ2]
That passion spans the entire state. From Bend to Astoria, Medford to Pendleton and everywhere between, fans drive hours to see games, listen on radio and internet, and gather for watch parties—turning game nights into community celebrations. Rip City is Oregon’s team.
And it starts young. A child’s first shirt, a family’s first trip to Moda, generations watching together on rainy winter nights—these moments create lifelong fans. Moving the team wouldn’t just break a business relationship; it would break family traditions and community bonds built over decades.
Recently, the Rip City Forever coalition called on government and civic leaders to do their part to keep the Blazers in Portland. We support their efforts. But this pledge is different—it’s fans speaking directly to prospective owners, from the people who buy the tickets, wear the jerseys, and bring the energy every night. This isn’t politics, it’s pride—and a promise to keep Rip City strong.
I've written the following Pledge to the next owners. If you agree, add your name to my petition now >> [https://secure.ngpvan.com/oLJJz4nOS0Krn4TukSVmxQ2]
Our Pledge to You, the Next Owners
* We’ll show up: filling the arena, making noise, and bringing Rip City energy every night.

* We’ll invest: buying tickets, jerseys, and supporting local businesses connected to the team.

* We’ll grow with you: embracing your vision and helping build the future here in Portland.

* We’ll share our stories: posting photos and memories with #RipCityStays , showing the passion and history of this fan base.

* We’ll bring new fans: taking friends and family to their first Blazers game and hosting watch parties across Oregon—proving Rip City is alive far beyond Portland.

* We’ll support local businesses: eating, shopping, and gathering downtown on game days—showing that the Blazers are an economic engine for Portland and all of Oregon.

* We’ll support community impact: volunteering and giving through Blazers Foundation initiatives that make our region stronger.
Let's send a message to the future owners that we're ready for this next chapter in the Trail Blazers Story >> [https://secure.ngpvan.com/oLJJz4nOS0Krn4TukSVmxQ2]
SIGN THE PLEDGE >> [https://secure.ngpvan.com/oLJJz4nOS0Krn4TukSVmxQ2]
All we ask in return: Keep the Blazers in Portland.
Other cities have passionate fans, but Portland’s devotion is different: a statewide community where generations rally behind the same jersey, and where “Rip City” has grown into part of Oregon’s identity. From the sellout streak to the iconic memories, this isn’t just support—it’s a relationship passed down through families.
As the 50th anniversary of Portland's NBA Championship approaches, we're ready to celebrate the past and build the future—with you, right here in Portland.
We’re all in. Keep Rip City in Portland. #RipCityStays
Thank you,
Tobias Read
Oregon Secretary of State
CONTRIBUTE [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/read-em-24?refcode=250815EM-blazers]
https://www.facebook.com/TobiasReadOR [https://www.facebook.com/TobiasReadOR] https://twitter.com/TobiasRead [https://twitter.com/TobiasRead] https://instagram.com/tobiasreador [https://instagram.com/tobiasreador]

Friends of Tobias Read (5208)
PO Box 42307
Portland, OR 97242
United States
If you believe you received this message in error or wish to no longer receive email from us, please unsubscribe: https://secure.ngpvan.com/p/IMahE5FQZEyEjGesjn_XSA2?unsubscribedata=YXsbR%2FAPeQZz0HM9Xn7pL7W%2FO0TWSRHRlVw5kJd4ly1lXUnWwWO%2Fapp5%2BVqLh7EL78dCu07GwI2708hhNGPMlV%2B8aFgOOXTrM5tm6AjK4GH11%2Flv6l3RXSonYgDSuYQCEDfRo2FKjGRSbn4ovDJk3xGYvoubWZOlx28iYBjclusxOGxnijPtTsEag1E1BB%2BZmXJr53FekkZI4yg91fs7dGPbW%2B%2Fwx3BRZNV44IPZ%2FcJ1cdDvPlJwcBkepY5A%2FMmRZJLz%2Bu%2Fr2hozF7TbdyPxavPfPEN0aPiTwpn60hgMC7DZGi20%2BmHUC9xuPPfz7i5htUkZ3BYYUrII5nTiDgL0RzVPBkeDkywADCypl1gvPXHIbWiwlsSlFggz9MO53srnY%2B3oBPRm8rwaKitgdCJisMvmOWBZi4BS%2FSVb6t94JuvRulLPz1JDyZl5LlnZdHWF .

body:
Democrats are gaining ground in these critically important races.
#

**_Three quick facts about three GOP-held U.S. Senate seats Democrats need to flip to restore our majority in 2026_** **[FLIP THE SENATE: CHIP IN](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/93850?refcode=em20250815-9286&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1&akid=9286%2E614464%2Ezpye6o)**
---

**— North Carolina —** After GOP incumbent Thom Tillis announced his intent to retire in 2026, Cook Political Report UPGRADED North Carolina’s U.S. Senate race from “Lean Republican” to “TOSS UP!” **[FLIP THIS SEAT: CHIP IN](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/93850?refcode=em20250815-9286&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=2&akid=9286%2E614464%2Ezpye6o)**
---

**— Texas —** Republican infighting in Texas has left its reliably GOP-held Senate seat uniquely vulnerable, resulting in an UPGRADE from “Solid Republican” to “Likely Republican” by Cook Political Report. **[FLIP THIS SEAT: CHIP IN](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/93850?refcode=em20250815-9286&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=3&akid=9286%2E614464%2Ezpye6o)**
---

**— Ohio —** With news of former U.S. Senator Sherrod Brown officially confirming his intention to run for the seat, the Center for Politics UPGRADED Ohio’s Senate race from “Likely Republican” to “Lean Republican.” **[FLIP THIS SEAT: CHIP IN](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/93850?refcode=em20250815-9286&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=4&akid=9286%2E614464%2Ezpye6o)**
---

**_While these shifts might seem small right now, they indicate that Democrats are gaining ground to take back our Senate majority in 2026 — even more than a year out from the election!_**
